target_game.name: Block Blast！
target_game.core_loop: Drag polyomino blocks onto an 8x8 grid to complete full rows or columns, clearing them to score points and free up space.
target_game.audience_proxy: Casual mobile gamers aged 25-55 seeking a relaxing, low-pressure mental challenge during short breaks.
target_game.visual_style: Clean, vibrant abstract aesthetic featuring glossy, beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid.

palette.description: A soothing royal blue background paired with a dark navy game board and bright, saturated block colors.

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · live-action-UGC

centroid_hook.visual_action: A person in an olive jacket and tan pants performs a rhythmic dance in a dimly lit room.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: how I feel after playing Crossword Go all night after school

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 10d. Hook representative: "A young man dances energetically to express his positive mood after playing Cros"

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · in-game

centroid_hook.visual_action: A small red fruit is dropped from the top, hits other fruits, and merges into a larger green melon.

top_archetypes.rationale: 2 creatives across 2 unique advertisers, average age 112d. Hook representative: "A fruit-merging game where a small fruit is dropped into a container full of lar"

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · mixed

centroid_hook.summary: Hands dragging jigsaw puzzle pieces on a smartphone screen.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A close-up shot of a person's hands interacting with a puzzle app on a white iPhone.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 117d. Hook representative: "Hands dragging jigsaw puzzle pieces on a smartphone screen."

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · 3D-render

centroid_hook.summary: Opening a drawer and filling a spice jar with red pepper flakes.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A 3D animated drawer opens, and red pepper flakes pour into a jar labeled 'RED PEPPER'.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 143d. Hook representative: "Opening a drawer and filling a spice jar with red pepper flakes."

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Goku Ultra Instinct charging a powerful energy blast.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Goku in his Ultra Instinct form charges a glowing blue Kamehameha against a flashing rainbow background.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 619d. Hook representative: "Goku Ultra Instinct charging a powerful energy blast."

game_fit_scores.rationale: Major friction: this is a live-action UGC dance reaction tied to a word game (Crossword Go), with an earthy muted palette and a "post-school all-nighter" framing that skews younger/student — misaligned with Block Blast's 25-55 casual relaxer audience and its glossy vibrant blue/gold abstract look. The hook also shows zero gameplay, so it fails to communicate the spatial drag-and-drop line-clear loop that drives Block Blast installs. Only worth testing if reshot with a 25-45 talent, swapped overlay to "how I feel after clearing a 4-line combo," and a picture-in-picture or cutaway to actual 8x8 grid gameplay; otherwise skip — cluster is also tiny (n=1) and unproven.

Hard pass on a literal adaptation: the warm cream/red/brown fruit palette and physics-merge container directly contradict Block Blast's cool blue grid aesthetic and grid-based line-clear loop, creating a bait-and-switch risk that will tank IPM/D1 retention. The only transferable element is the broader "oddly satisfying drop-and-cascade" satisfaction beat, which does overlap with the 25-55 casual puzzle audience — so consider borrowing the ASMR pacing and chain-reaction payoff structure but rebuild it natively around polyomino drops triggering multi-line clears. Do not run the fruit creative as-is; misleading-ads policy risk on iOS is also non-trivial.

The "satisfying hands dragging puzzle pieces" hook taps into the same casual 25-55 puzzler audience and shares a drag-and-drop tactile language, so the audience overlap is solid. However, the visual frictions are significant: jigsaw pieces are not polyominoes, the white/red/blue member palette clashes with Block Blast's signature royal-blue + dark-navy + gold board, and there's no 8x8 grid or line-clear payoff in the centroid hook. Recommend testing only if reshot with actual Block Blast gameplay (real grid, branded palette, line-clear satisfaction beat) using the "hands-on-phone" framing as a wrapper—otherwise the creative will mislead users and tank IPM. Also note the thin cluster evidence (1 creative, 1 advertiser, 117d old) means this is a low-confidence signal, not a proven winner.

The "satisfying organization" 3D-render hook lives in a totally different visual world from Block Blast — pastel kitchen tones vs. saturated blocks on a navy grid — and there's no natural bridge from pouring spices into the drag-and-drop line-clear loop without a heavy-handed "blocks pour into grid" reframe. Audience has partial overlap (the same 25-55 casual scrollers enjoy ASMR-organization content), but with velocity 0.21 and a single advertiser behind it, this archetype isn't worth a bespoke production unless we test a low-cost adaptation that literally translates the "pour into container" satisfaction beat into blocks cascading to clear a near-full board. Pass on a faithful clone; only greenlight if creative can hard-marry the satisfaction trigger to the line-clear payoff in the first 2 seconds.

Hard pass for Block Blast. The hook leans on copyrighted Dragon Ball IP (legal/ASA risk) and a high-energy shonen anime aesthetic with neon cyan/magenta/yellow that clashes with the game's calm royal-blue, beveled-block visual identity — there is zero connection to drag-and-drop line-clear mechanics. Audience also skews young male anime fans rather than the 25-55 casual puzzle demo, and the cluster is a single stale creative (619d, n=1) with no proven scale signal. Do not adapt.

brief.title: "How I Feel After a 4-Line Combo" — Block Blast Satisfaction UGC
brief.hook_3s: POV: it's 11pm, phone glow on your face, thumb hovers over a perfect L-block — and the whole grid is about to vanish.
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Tight selfie-style shot of a 30-something woman on the couch, royal-blue phone glow lighting her face, eyebrows raised in quiet focus. Overlay teases the satisfaction payoff.
3-7s GAMEPLAY CUT-IN: Vertical phone screen-record fills the frame — finger drags a yellow polyomino into the last gap on the 8x8 navy grid. Hold for the snap.

7-10s PAYOFF: Four rows clear simultaneously with a gold particle burst and combo counter spiking; cut back to her doing a tiny seated shoulder-shimmy / fist-pump — understated adult satisfaction, not a teen dance.
10-13s RELATABILITY BEAT: She exhales, smiles, leans back into the couch. Quick montage of two more clears (gem collected, star bar filling).
13-15s CTA: End card — Block Blast logo on royal blue, gold 'PLAY FREE' button pulsing once.
brief.visual_direction: Live-action UGC shot vertically on a phone, but lit and graded to echo Block Blast's DNA: the dominant ambient light is the royal blue (#5469B9) of the phone screen washing over the talent, set against a dark navy (#1E2744) room. Gameplay cut-ins use the game's native glossy, beveled blocks on the high-contrast dark grid, with gold (#FFD700) accents on combos, stars, and the CTA button to create a consistent color trio across live action and screen capture. Mood is calm-satisfying, not hype — micro-reactions over big dance moves, matching the 25-45 relaxer audience.
text_overlays: the gap is exactly an L-shape…
brief.rationale: Keeps the proven satisfaction-UGC structure but fixes every flagged friction: ages talent up to 25-45, swaps the dance for a subtle adult micro-reaction, and front-loads actual 8x8 drag-and-drop + line-clear gameplay so the hook earns the install. Color-grade the live action to match the #5469B9/#1E2744/#FFD700 palette so live and gameplay cuts feel like one creative. Test against a control with no gameplay cut-in to isolate whether the relatability hook or the combo payoff is doing the work.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, 9:16 vertical mobile ad still: close-up of a 35-year-old woman's face on a dark couch at night, lit entirely by the royal blue (#5469B9) glow of her phone screen, dark navy (#1E2744) room behind her, a soft gold (#FFD700) reflection in her eyes from an in-game combo, subtle satisfied half-smile. Phone screen visible at bottom edge showing glossy beveled multi-colored blocks on a high-contrast dark grid in the clean vibrant abstract aesthetic of Block Blast. Bold white sans-serif overlay text top-center: 'me at 11pm, one more round'. Photorealistic UGC selfie look, shallow depth of field, grainy phone-camera texture.

Storyboard frame 2, 9:16 vertical: extreme close-up screen-record POV of a thumb dragging a yellow L-shaped polyomino toward the final gap on an 8x8 navy (#1E2744) grid, surrounding cells filled with glossy beveled blocks in royal blue (#5469B9), red, green, and gold (#FFD700). Clean vibrant abstract aesthetic featuring glossy, beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid. Gold particle sparkles anticipating the clear. Large overlay text centered: 'the gap is exactly an L-shape…'. Crisp UI, mobile screen recording style.

Storyboard frame 3, 9:16 vertical: split-screen — top half shows the same woman doing a small celebratory shoulder-shimmy on her couch, bathed in royal blue (#5469B9) phone light against a dark navy (#1E2744) background; bottom half shows the Block Blast 8x8 grid mid-clear with four rows exploding into gold (#FFD700) particles, glossy beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid, clean vibrant abstract aesthetic. Bold gold overlay text across the seam: '4-LINE COMBO 🔥'. Energetic but cozy mood, vertical mobile ad composition.

brief.title: Hands-On Satisfaction: One Perfect Clear
brief.hook_3s: Close-up: a thumb drags a glowing gold polyomino toward a nearly-full 8x8 navy grid — one block away from a massive multi-line clear.
scene_flow: 0–3s HOOK: ASMR close-up of a hand holding a phone in portrait. The 8x8 dark-navy Block Blast grid fills the screen. Three bright polyomino pieces wait at the bottom; rows are dangerously close to filling. Text snaps in: 'Can you clear it?'
3–7s TENSION: Thumb drags a blue L-piece into place — one row clears with a glossy shimmer and gold sparks. Score jumps. Camera pushes slightly closer. Overlay: 'One more move...'
7–11s PAYOFF: Thumb slots the final gold square piece. FOUR lines clear simultaneously in a cascading bevel-flash, gold stars burst, combo counter pops 'x4!'. Satisfying chime + haptic shimmer.
11–14s SOCIAL PROOF: Quick split: score rockets past a leaderboard number. Overlay: '97% can't beat this board.'
14–15s CTA: Logo lockup on royal blue, glossy gold button pulses. Overlay: 'Play Free.'
brief.visual_direction: Shot in vertical 9:16 with a real human hand (warm neutral skin tone, no jewelry) holding a clean white phone against a soft royal-blue (#5469B9) gradient backdrop — echoing the in-game palette so the device feels like a natural extension of the brand. The on-screen gameplay dominates 80% of frame: dark-navy (#1E2744) 8x8 board, glossy beveled polyominoes in saturated brand colors, and #FFD700 gold accents on score, stars, and clear-flashes. Camera uses subtle macro push-ins on each drop, with crisp tactile sound design (click, whoosh, sparkle). Mood is calm-then-triumphant — never frantic. Every line-clear must feel buttery, glossy, and inevitable.
brief.rationale: This adapts the proven 'hands-on-phone satisfaction' framing but reshoots it with authentic Block Blast gameplay — real 8x8 grid, brand palette, and a line-clear payoff — eliminating the IPM risk of misleading jigsaw visuals flagged in the fit scan. Lead with this as a low-spend test ($200–500/day) against 25–55 casual puzzle lookalikes; if CTR/IPM clear benchmark within 72 hours, scale and build variants swapping the hook question and combo size. Treat as low-confidence archetype evidence — cap budget until signal stabilizes.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, vertical 9:16 aspect ratio, photorealistic close-up of a human hand holding a white smartphone in portrait orientation against a soft royal blue #5469B9 gradient backdrop. On the phone screen: an 8x8 dark navy #1E2744 puzzle grid filled with glossy beveled polyomino blocks in saturated brand colors, a glowing gold #FFD700 L-shaped block hovering mid-drag above a nearly-complete row, gold sparks anticipating a line clear. Clean, vibrant abstract aesthetic featuring glossy, beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid. Bold white sans-serif on-screen text overlay reads 'Can you clear it?' at the top. Cinematic macro lens, soft key light, tactile and satisfying mood.

Storyboard frame 2, vertical 9:16 aspect ratio, dynamic close-up of a smartphone screen mid-action: four horizontal rows simultaneously clearing on an 8x8 dark navy #1E2744 grid, explosive gold #FFD700 sparks and beveled glossy block fragments cascading outward, score counter glowing gold in the top-right. Background royal blue #5469B9 bokeh. Clean, vibrant abstract aesthetic featuring glossy, beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid. Large punchy yellow-gold text overlay 'x4 COMBO!' centered with subtle motion blur. High-contrast, celebratory, ASMR-satisfying lighting.

Storyboard CTA frame, vertical 9:16 aspect ratio, clean end-card composition on a deep royal blue #5469B9 background with a centered glossy beveled Block Blast logo lockup, a single floating gold #FFD700 polyomino block and three sparkle stars, a pulsing gold rounded CTA button at the bottom. Dark navy #1E2744 subtle grid pattern fading in the background. Clean, vibrant abstract aesthetic featuring glossy, beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid. Bold white text overlay 'Play Free' on the button. Premium, calm, inviting finish.

brief.title: One Drop. Total Cascade.
brief.hook_3s: A glowing gold polyomino hovers over a nearly-full 8x8 grid — one tap drops it and detonates a rainbow chain-clear across 6 lines.
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Tight macro shot of a dark navy 8x8 grid packed with glossy beveled blocks, one perfect gold-glowing L-piece hovers above the only matching gap. ASMR hum builds.
3-7s TENSION: Finger drags the gold piece slowly into place; camera pushes in, blocks subtly pulse, soft click as it locks.
7-11s PAYOFF: Chain reaction — 3 rows clear, then 3 columns cascade, golden sparks and stars burst, score counter rockets from 12,400 to 48,900.
11-14s ESCALATION: Quick montage of 3 more satisfying multi-line clears with combo text popping ('x4 COMBO!', 'PERFECT!').
14-15s CTA: Logo lockup on royal blue, gold 'PLAY FREE' button pulses once.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Block Blast's native DNA: deep navy (#1E2744) board floating on a soothing royal blue (#5469B9) backdrop, glossy beveled polyominoes in saturated jewel tones, with #FFD700 gold reserved as the 'hero' accent for the decisive piece, sparks, stars, and CTA. Camera is locked top-down with subtle parallax pushes; lighting is soft studio with crisp specular highlights on each bevel. Pacing borrows the ASMR cadence of the fruit-merge archetype — slow inhale, satisfying click, explosive cascade — but every visual element is grid-native polyomino, zero physics/fruit cues, to avoid bait-and-switch.
text_overlays: Can you clear it in ONE move?
brief.rationale: This brief preserves the archetype's winning 'oddly satisfying drop → cascade payoff' beat that drove its IPM, but rebuilds it 100% natively in Block Blast's grid loop — eliminating misleading-ads risk on iOS while keeping the 25-55 casual satisfaction trigger intact. UA should A/B the gold-hero-piece variant against a non-gold control to isolate accent-driven CTR lift, and run it primarily on Facebook/TikTok where ASMR puzzle cascades over-index. Expect strongest pickup with women 35-54 — mirror the fruit archetype's audience overlap (68/100) without inheriting its visual mismatch penalty.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, mobile game ad still: top-down view of a dark navy #1E2744 8x8 grid nearly full of glossy beveled polyomino blocks in saturated jewel tones, one luminous golden #FFD700 L-shaped block hovering above with soft glow and particle sparks, royal blue #5469B9 background with subtle radial gradient, clean vibrant abstract aesthetic featuring glossy beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid, bold white sans-serif on-screen text 'Can you clear it in ONE move?' at top, cinematic studio lighting, crisp specular highlights, ultra-detailed, no characters

Storyboard frame 2, aspect 9:16, mid-action mobile puzzle ad still: same dark navy #1E2744 8x8 grid mid-explosion as 3 rows and 3 columns simultaneously clear in a rainbow cascade, golden #FFD700 sparks and stars bursting outward, royal blue #5469B9 backdrop, clean vibrant abstract aesthetic featuring glossy beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid, large yellow on-screen text 'CHAIN CLEAR! x4 COMBO' centered, motion blur on flying particles, satisfying glossy beveled block fragments, vivid saturated colors, no characters

Storyboard CTA frame, aspect 9:16, end-card mobile game ad: centered Block Blast-style logo on royal blue #5469B9 background, a single glossy beveled golden #FFD700 polyomino block resting on a small dark navy #1E2744 grid tile below, clean vibrant abstract aesthetic featuring glossy beveled blocks on a high-contrast dark grid, large pulsing gold button with bold white text 'PLAY FREE' at the bottom third, soft sparkle particles, premium polished mobile ad finish, no characters
